root_cause: AutoRound's Gemma4 MoE block-wise quantization has a bug in its position embedding caching: cached position_embeddings from layer 0 (seq_len=512) have 256-dim RoPE tensors, but subsequent layers' attention expects 512-dim RoPE tensors. The bug is in _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs which incorrectly caches/reuses per-layer rotary embeddings across layers.
suggested_fix: AutoRound's Gemma4 MoE + block-wise quantization with NVFP4 scheme is fundamentally incompatible with this model (gemma-4-A4B-98e-v6-coder-it). The root cause is in auto_round/compressors/special_model_handler.py _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs which caches position_embeddings at layer 0 with incorrect RoPE dimensions and reuses them for later layers. Retry will not help. Suggested alternatives: (1) Use llm_compressor format instead of auto_round for NVFP4, (2) Use W4A16 scheme instead of NVFP4 which uses full-model rather than block-wise quantization, (3) Use a different quantization tool such as GPTQModel or AWQ that does not use block-wise calibration with cached position embeddings.
